name: Issue ID tokens in the authorization code flow
outcome: An authorization code flow requesting the openid scope returns a signed, verifiable ID token alongside the access token. The ID token is added in AuthCodeGrant after makeBearerTokenResponse returns, never by editing AbstractGrant (which all five grants inherit).
scope: Add the ID token builder and wire it into AuthCodeGrant's token response, gated on OIDC being enabled and the openid scope being granted. Tag the OIDC access token with typ:at+jwt. Auto-recognize the standard OIDC scopes. Add a docs callout for this issue's behavior changes (no docs-bundle deferral).
acceptance_criteria: An authorization code flow with scope=openid returns a signed id_token alongside the access token, added in AuthCodeGrant.respondToAccessTokenRequest after makeBearerTokenResponse — AbstractGrant.makeBearerTokenResponse is NOT modified
BearerTokenResponse.body.id_token type is explicitly added (optional string) — call out the type-shape change as a noted public-API extension
The id_token verifies against the JWKS key and carries correct iss, sub, aud, exp, iat, conditional nonce/auth_time, and at_hash

at_hash is pinned to SHA-256 (correct for RS256) — left-half base64url of SHA-256(access_token) — and the unit test asserts the exact value against the OIDC Core §A.3 known-answer test vector, not just presence

Nonce single-use enforcement (consequence test, not a new mechanism): replaying a redeemed authorization code returns invalid_grant and issues no ID token. Documented as the relying party's responsibility for nonce replay across distinct codes
sub is produced by the shared canonical stringify (String(user.id)) and aud is the client id; sub is byte-identical to the eventual UserInfo sub (issue 7 uses the same helper)

When OIDC is enabled the access token JOSE header carries typ:at+jwt (RFC 9068) and the id_token header carries typ:JWT; the at+jwt header is applied by threading sign() options through this.encrypt() — currently AbstractGrant.encryptAccessToken calls this.encrypt with no options
A flow without the openid scope returns no id_token; non-OIDC flows are byte-identical (HS256 JOSE-header snapshot from issue 1 still passes)
Documentation step: add an OIDC ID-token reference page section to the docs site as part of this issue, not deferred to issue 9
steps: src/oidc — add a shared canonical subject stringify helper (String(user.id)) used by the ID token builder; issues 6 and 7 reuse the same helper so sub is byte-identical everywhere

src/oidc — add an ID token builder assembling protocol claims (iss, sub via the shared stringify helper, aud set to the client, exp from the access-token TTL, iat, conditional nonce and auth_time) and at_hash with SHA-256 pinned
src/oidc — add a known-answer at_hash unit test against OIDC Core §A.3: access_token = 'jHkWEdUXMU1BwAsC4vtUsZwnNvTIxEl0z9K3vx5KF0Y', at_hash = 'xsZZrUssMXjL3FBlzoSh2g' (cross-check before locking)

AbstractGrant — auto-recognize the standard OIDC scopes (openid, profile, email, address, phone) as valid when OIDC is enabled. offline_access is intentionally NOT auto-recognized in v1 — refresh-flow OIDC handling is consumer-owned, so advertising offline_access would mislead RPs (drop is a Should-Fix per PLAN_REVIEW; apply here for clean v1 semantics)

AuthCodeGrant.respondToAccessTokenRequest — after makeBearerTokenResponse returns, mint the id_token (when OIDC is on and openid was granted) and attach it to the bearer response body. Do NOT modify AbstractGrant.makeBearerTokenResponse — the other four grants inherit it and would silently change behavior

AbstractGrant.encryptAccessToken — thread SignOptions through this.encrypt → this.jwt.sign so the OIDC code path can set the JOSE header { typ: 'at+jwt' }. Non-OIDC consumers pass no options and continue to get { alg:HS256, typ:JWT } byte-identical headers
index.ts — export the ID token claim types
test/unit — claim assembly, at_hash against the OIDC Core §A.3 KAT, the openid-scope gate, the typ:at+jwt access-token header, and the BearerTokenResponse.body.id_token presence/absence
test/e2e — nonce-single-use replay test: redeem an auth code once, replay it, assert invalid_grant and no id_token in the response
docs site — add the ID token reference content (claim set, hooks, scope-gating) as part of this issue, not deferred
notes: ID token aud is the client, deliberately different from the access token's resource aud. exp reuses the access-token TTL — no separate config knob. azp is omitted in v1 (valid only while aud is single-valued [client_id]; documented in CONTEXT.md and the docs page). The ID token stays lean: protocol claims only. The OIDC access token is tagged typ:at+jwt so the AccessTokenVerifier seam (issue 2) can reject an ID token presented as a bearer token — see ADR 0003. OAuthException additions moved to issue 1 to avoid the issue 4/6 merge conflict.
